name: Hyatt Place Madison/Downtown
address: 333 W Washington Ave, Madison, WI 53703, USA
strengths: exceptionally friendly and helpful staff
hearty and varied complimentary breakfast
prime downtown location near state capitol
weaknesses: sagging mattresses affecting sleep quality
missing in-room amenities like microwave in some suites
paid parking at $20/night adds to cost
ai_summary: The Hyatt Place Madison/Downtown earns strong marks for its warm, attentive staff and generous breakfast offering in an unbeatable downtown location. Minor maintenance issues such as sagging mattresses and inconsistent room amenities keep it from reaching its full potential.

reviews.raw_text:
In town for a game on campus. It was a pretty decent experience. The location was close enough where walking was excellent. The hotel itself is pretty good. Rooms were clean and beds were comfy, although the mattresses were definitely sagging towards the middle, so it made for some awkward sleep.

Breakfast was okay. Very busy on weekend mornings but stocked well enough. Parking was $20/night and included on the final bill. Can’t complain there either.

Overall a decent hotel stay and I’ll look here for staying again if I’m ever near the UW campus area.
complaints: mattresses sagging towards the middle
breakfast very busy on weekend mornings

reviews.raw_text:
We stayed 2 nights at the Hyatt for my son's surgery at UW children's.

The staff was very friendly, super helpful during breakfast. Matteo especially had good jokes 😂

The rooms were clean and quiet. Breakfast was practically a feast and included eggs, bacon, French toast, bagels, cereal, oatmeal, muffins, milk and oat milk, coffee, juice. It's literally down the street from the capital building so it was a nice little walk to the capital. There's a grocery store within a block or two if you need snacks. There's coffee shops and restaurants nearby. It's a 10 minute drive from UW. It was convenient for us and a pleasant experience in a stressful situation.
compliments: staff was very friendly and super helpful
convenient location near capital and UW
pleasant experience in a stressful situation

reviews.raw_text: I don’t always go out of my way to leave a long review but this hotel deserves more than five stars. The staff is wonderful, from the college students at the front desk to the managers on duty, everyone is just happy to be there. I forgot my AirPods on the bed because they were white and blended with the bedsheets and I called to report them lost. They went out of their way to search for it and then mailed it to me  right away. The experience was already a positive one before this happened, but I am so happy that our group (Galena Crew) stayed here. If we ever have a show in Madison again, we know EXACTLY where to go now!
compliments: staff is wonderful from front desk to managers
everyone is happy to be there
staff went out of their way to search for lost AirPods

reviews.raw_text: Disappointed. I’ve stayed here before and this time very disappointed. We stayed here several days in a suite, 2 queen beds and pull out. There was no microwave. Is it possible we were the only room in the entire hotel who didn’t have one. We were there Thursday to Sunday. I don’t know if you need to request housekeeping. Everyone very nice except an older women, shoulder length bob, grayish hair, crossbody bag. Worked afternoons/evenings. What an unfriendly person. Perhaps she had a few bad days??
complaints: no microwave in suite
unfriendly front desk staff member with shoulder length bob and gray hair working afternoons/evenings
compliments: everyone very nice except one staff member

reviews.raw_text:
This is a perfect location if you're exploring the state capitol. Depending on what side of the building you're on you could probably see it, but we didn't get that view. It was still gorgeous, with a great sight of the city.

The breakfast was good, they switch some of the items, so while on the first morning we ate sausage and pancakes with the other food, the next day had bacon and French toast with scrambled eggs, toast/bagel/english muffin, hardboiled eggs, yogurt with different toppings like fruit, granola, nuts. Cereals. The only thing missing was potatoes, but it was a solid free breakfast.

The room was clean, didn't find any hair in the shower or sink,  everything looked clean. The only downside was there's no microwave. They only have microwaves in the top floors of the hotel, floors 9+. We were on the 8th floor.
complaints: no microwave on 8th floor
microwaves only available on floors 9+
missing potatoes from breakfast
compliments: breakfast was good with variety of items
room was clean with no hair in shower or sink

reviews.raw_text: We loved staying here for our wedding. The staff were super friendly and helpful! They made it easy to set up our hotel block. The rooms were clean, quiet, and beautiful. Our wedding guests were very happy, too. We would absolutely stay here again.
compliments: staff were super friendly and helpful
rooms were clean, quiet, and beautiful
made it easy to set up hotel block

reviews.raw_text: While the hotel is amazing, the service is what really made our stay there outstanding. Someone needs to give Cromwell Ramos a raise. He went above and beyond to ensure we had a pleasant time in Madison. Would absolutely stay here again.
compliments: service is what really made our stay outstanding
Cromwell Ramos went above and beyond

reviews.raw_text:
The Hilton Garden Inn Madison Downtown is not only an incredible hotel, but the service experience will wish you could stay longer.
I arrived after a long series of flights from Europe, and I literally dragged myself from my Uber to the front desk. The two gentleman behind the desk were amazingly funny, uplifting, and clearly focused on making my experience at this Hilton Garden Inn positive and uplifting.
Neil is the best.  He and his colleague presented the Prize Wheel that I was able to spin for prizes, and I won a free breakfast!  After 22 hours of travel with very little sleep, I made my way to my room with a smile on my face and a realization that there is something very special about this particular Hilton Garden Inn.
For Hilton Management: I taught marketing for six years at the university level.  You should study how this team was trained, motivated, and supported.  They are so positive and customer focused.
I’ve stayed 83 nights at Hilton properties so far this year, both in
compliments: two gentleman behind desk were amazingly funny and uplifting
won free breakfast via Prize Wheel
staff are so positive and customer focused
something very special about this Hilton Garden Inn

reviews.raw_text: Wonderful. I was there because my wife was hospitalized, and the staff couldn't have been more helpful during a stressful time. Best staff members were Cromwell, Maddie, Jai, Kenneth, and Dayton. One not so great was a woman working breakfast in the restaurant. Not sure of her name, but it was something like Vrisa. Overall, such a wonderful hotel stay. Finally, order anything you want from the menu without hesitation, because the food is fabulous there. Hotel is clean and modern. You won't be sorry if you book a visit at the Hilton Garden Inn.

compliments: staff couldn't have been more helpful during stressful time

reviews.raw_text: Love staying at this hotel. Nice location,  very clean, and well-appointed.  They were slow in getting our rooms ready, so we had to check into one later. The staff were very nice.
complaints: slow in getting rooms ready

ai_summary: Hotel Ruby Marie is a characterful 150-year-old boutique hotel offering strong value through its inclusive perks — free parking, breakfast, and drinks — in a prime downtown Madison location. The main drawback is an unavoidable early-morning train that passes nearby, which can significantly disrupt sleep for light sleepers.

reviews.raw_text: Its a cool old hotel. Been around for 150 yrs. I was hoping for haunting but no such luck. I liked the bar downstairs. Live music vocals and guitar. It was so nice. There's a continental breakfast. Hot and delicious. Quiet and you can watch the sun come up from the lake while you eat breakfast. So nice to sit in the warm sunshine There's a Cafe 2 doors down. Cheap and homemade deliciousness 😋  I will stay there again. 🥰
compliments: Live music vocals and guitar was so nice
Quiet location with lake view from breakfast area

reviews.raw_text:
I had a great stay at Ruby Marie Hotel. The rooms are nicely designed, and everything feels thoughtfully curated. The location is perfect, especially if you want to explore Vienna easily.
A special thank you to Timo and Ivan, who truly made the experience exceptional. They were incredibly friendly and professional.
compliments: Rooms are nicely designed and thoughtfully curated
Staff members Timo and Ivan were incredibly friendly and professional

reviews.raw_text:
We absolutely loved this place! It's very clean, comfy beds, affordable, free parking within a 10 minute walk to the square, two complimentary beverages, breakfast/brunch included. My only complaint is that we arrived just shortly after 7:00, and couldn't figure out how to check in. (Saturday night).
So go to the Up North Pub to get your keys if you are confused. Highly recommend!!!
complaints: Confused check-in process on Saturday night after 7:00pm
compliments: Affordable with free parking within 10 minute walk

reviews.raw_text: There are several challenges with this hotel. First and foremost, which oddly, no one has mentioned is that there is a train that goes right by it at 4:30am, and again at 5am. So, although the location is “convenient” to downtown Madison, it’s terrible in terms of a good night’s sleep. They are clearly trying to overcome this with free drinks at the bar downstairs, parking and breakfast included. For us, those “perks” did not overcome the lack of sleep. As soon as you enter the reception area, it does not smell good—perhaps due to years of being above the bar below? The room itself was OK, but smelled kind of musty, perhaps due to the leaking HVAC, which resulted in wet carpeting. We told the receptionist about it and requested another room. We were told they had no other rooms free, which could have been the case although we saw no other guests during our two nights there. The receptionist just put down some towels on the floor, which of course did nothing to solve the problem. We did
complaints: Train passes by at 4:30am and 5am causing sleep disruption
Reception area smells bad possibly from bar below
Room smelled musty due to leaking HVAC and wet carpeting
Staff placed towels on floor instead of providing alternative room

reviews.raw_text: The location is great, coms with free parking and easy walk to the Capitol and restaurants in the area. We love architectural gems and this is one! The rooms reflect the bygone days of the hotel (1800s) while having the modern conveniences we expect (mini fridge, en suite bathroom). Good water pressure and hot water (important to us). Very quiet at night in our courtyard room (except when the train went thru town!) Joshua and Amber were so kind and helpful. Give it a try!
complaints: Train goes through town at times
compliments: Great location with free parking near Capitol and restaurants
Architectural gem from 1800s with modern conveniences
Good water pressure and hot water

ai_summary: The Saddlery Madison delivers an upscale, apartment-style experience with spotless rooms, a stellar central location, and a management team praised universally for warmth and flexibility. It is particularly well-suited for extended stays, remote workers, and guests seeking a homey alternative to traditional hotels in Madison.

reviews.raw_text: We stayed at the Saddlery for six weeks in September/October 2025 and LOVED it. It was fantastic. Sam and the team at the Saddlery run a great place and are very friendly and always quick to answer questions and help out with anything. Our unit had everything we needed, including washer/dryer and a fully stocked kitchen, allowing us to make many meals in our unit -- which we often ate out on the rooftop deck overlooking the lake. Our unit was on the 3rd floor with a gorgeous view of the lake. There's a gym (also with a view of the lake!) in the basement, and a secure bike locker area, which was great since we rode our bikes everywhere. The location couldn't be better-- we walked or biked to many restaurants, the Farmer's Market, everything on campus, the grocery store, and more. They cleaned our unit weekly, and if we ever ran out of anything (towels, dishwasher detergent, toilet paper, etc), there's an amenity closet on the main floor where we could easily restock. It was a perfect ho
compliments: Sam and the team are very friendly and quick to answer questions
Unit had everything needed including washer/dryer and fully stocked kitchen
Weekly cleaning and convenient amenity closet for restocking supplies

reviews.raw_text: Great and friendly management.  Very responsive.  Innovative business model using text to communicate.   Great location.  Super clean and nicely furnished rooms.   We stayed there to help our adult child recuperate after an organ donation at UW Health.  Because the dates can be iffy with transplants, the management worked with us to adjust our timing without penalty to accommodate shifting procedure dates.  We'll be back!
compliments: Great and friendly management, very responsive
Innovative business model using text communication
Super clean and nicely furnished rooms

reviews.raw_text:
Stayed at a loft with a king size bed in it's own room, with a living room and kitchenette and a view of the lake. The room was very spacious, not to mention beautiful and homey.

I had hit a couple of snags due to an unfortunate incident with Cloudbeds, but the service at the Saddlery themselves was great and very accommodating to what happened.

Please note that there is no entry without a pass key to all entry points! Keep an eye on your emails and texts as they pass this along to you!

Overall great little place, short term or long term!
complaints: No entry without pass key to entry points
compliments: Spacious, beautiful, and homey loft with king bed and living room
Service at the Saddlery was great and very accommodating

reviews.raw_text:
A Perfect Blend of Luxury, Comfort, and a Stunning Lakeview

My stay at Saddlery Madison was exceptional from start to finish. I booked the 1‑bedroom King Suite, and it truly felt like an upscale condominium rather than a typical hotel room.

The suite is spacious and thoughtfully designed, with a full kitchen that made cooking effortless, an en suite office perfect for remote work, and a full washer and dryer right inside the unit (personal laundry room)— a huge advantage for both short and long stays. The king bedroom is serene and incredibly comfortable, making it easy to unwind.

One of the highlights of my stay was the beautiful Lakeview from my room. Waking up to that peaceful, scenic backdrop added a level of calm and luxury that made the entire experience feel elevated.

Saddlery Madison strikes the ideal balance between privacy, comfort, and convenience. Whether you’re staying for a few nights or settling in long‑term, it’s the perfect home away from home. I would absolutely s
compliments: Full kitchen, en suite office, washer and dryer in unit
King bedroom is serene and incredibly comfortable
Beautiful lakeview providing peaceful, scenic backdrop

reviews.raw_text: This hotel exceeded all our expectations! The location is absolutely perfect. We could walk to all the amazing things downtown Madison offers – the farmers market, fabulous restaurants, State Street shopping, and the UW – within minutes, yet the hotel itself felt like a peaceful retreat away from the hustle and bustle. The facilities are truly exceptional, with tastefully decorated and modern rooms, a full fitness center, a rooftop deck, and beautiful views of Lake Monona. And the attention to detail in maintaining the facilities was evident – everything was spotlessly clean and in perfect working order. Sam and the rest of the staff were always friendly and accessible if we needed anything. We couldn't have asked for a better combination of location and amenities. This hotel is perfect for both leisure and business travelers. We'll definitely be returning!
compliments: Perfect location walkable to farmers market, restaurants, and UW
Full fitness center and rooftop deck with beautiful lake views
Spotlessly clean facilities and friendly, accessible staff led by Sam

ai_summary: The Edgewater offers a picturesque Lake Mendota setting with generally clean, attractive rooms and moments of genuinely impressive personalized service. However, high prices, unreliable amenities, hidden charges, and maintenance lapses undercut the luxury experience guests expect at this rate.

reviews.raw_text: The rooms, hotel, and location are all beautiful. However, the parking is absurdly expensive, and the drivers who are supposed to drive you to places around the city, to alleviate the aforementioned absurd prices, are seldom available. Also, room service is liable not to come, even if you request it at the front desk. They will charge you an extra 50 bucks at the end and not tell you. It will just appear on your bill, which is even more absurd because housekeeping never came to my room during the entirety of my stay despite requesting it twice at the front desk.
complaints: parking is absurdly expensive
drivers seldom available for city transportation
room service fails to arrive despite requests
housekeeping never came despite two requests

reviews.raw_text: I am thoroughly impressed with the Edgewater. We booked a room and spa services for my friend's retirement celebration....on what was probably the coldest day of the year. To my surprise, Ryan greeted us, unloaded and parked the car, and coordinated a driver for our evening events. Chase was our driver to her party and dinner, and delivered her gifts to our room. Tiffany was my massage therapist for spa services the next day. Her technique was tailored to my comfort and was the perfect way to end our stay. Everyone we interacted with made us feel welcome and genuinely seemed to enjoy their role. Customer service is a tough career. There are some really great people that made our stay a complete delight. We'll be back!!
compliments: Ryan greeted us and coordinated driver services
Chase delivered gifts and provided excellent driving service
Tiffany's massage technique was tailored and perfect
staff made us feel welcome and genuinely enjoyed their roles

reviews.raw_text: Staff is very kind and professional and location is convenient and pretty (on the lake). Rooms are clean. Rates are high for what it is, comparable to other places in the area. There isn't a pool outside of the spa and spa prices are high for Madison, too. I booked erroneously under the wrong rate (totally my fault) but wasn't able to cancel as it was within 24-hours and they couldn't make any adjustments to figure out a half-way solution- so not super accomodating management.

reviews.raw_text: The room temperature was uncomfortably high; fortunately, we were able to ventilate the room by opening the windows. I ordered the sparkling wine for $68, which was unfortunately served in two glasses that were visibly soiled with lipstick stains. This is unacceptable, particularly given the occasion of a 25-year anniversary. No compensation or replacement glassware was offered. I have experienced superior accommodations and service at lower-priced hotels. While I generally enjoyed my stay, please be advised that there are additional daily charges for parking and various other fees.
complaints: room temperature was uncomfortably high

reviews.raw_text:
The rooms are spacious and clean, overall it's quiet. I wouldn't call the breakfast great, but it is there and it is hot. I'm disappointed that I called in advance to ask questions and one of those questions was is there a pool, and they said yes, but it's been out of service my entire trip. The activity I came to this city to do requires being on my feet for 10+ hours,.and since there is a shower but no tub, I had hoped to relax in the hot tub, perhaps take a swim. I bought a swimsuit specifically for this. Not a single day of this trip has it been open which is annoying.

Also important to note: no safe in the room.
complaints: pool out of service entire trip despite being told it was available

reviews.raw_text: Stayed for 1 night last week. Front desk staff was fantastic. Very happy with the complimentary water and very satisfied with parking. Was worried since I drive a truck but there was more than enough space! And there were about 5-6 electrical outlets on the desk! Wow!
compliments: more than enough parking space for truck

reviews.raw_text: Could not be happier with our stay. This was my first time staying in a hotel with my dog as well and they were so accommodating! Thank you especially to Mike and Sylvia!!

reviews.raw_text: Came for the MSU vs Wisconsin Hockey game. Locations is perfect quick walk to the game even with the snow! Andre at the front desk is amazing, top notch service. It was late at night and weather was terrible out and 1 year old wanted milk to get back to bed, the market in lobby was out but Andre jumped in to action and I had two cups of milk within the matter of minutes without asking! We are ready to book again to come back for the game next year!

reviews.raw_text:
Dropping A friend after a bowling tournament, my car was parked while they were checking in in the section where it’s supposed to be parked, taking up one single lane like any normal person would when they’re letting someone check in and I was told I had to move my car because I was blocking everything.  He was very rude and disrespectful. He could have easily said that we just need to pull around to the other side. But that would have meant he was trying to be kind. They didn’t even try to understand why I was even parked there. We were literally standing at the counter, checking in. I cannot recommend this hotel. That guy was completely rude. No use for it.
As far as front desk staff impeccable. Kind. Polite. I cannot comment on anything else. That was as far as I got.

Even though check-in is at 3:00pm, we were allowed to check in early, which was very much appreciated, because we could then park at the hotel and didn't have to go looking for parking elsewhere for the 3:00pm basketball game.

Great location. Great staff. Excellent service. Breakfast is really great and filling. They can make something for you if you don't like what is on the buffet. reviews.raw_text: Stayed here on December 24th Christmas Eve. The desk clerk was very nice and helped us get checked in. She told us about the shuttle service and various food options they had since their restaurant was closed due to it being a holiday. Our room was very clean and nice. We decided to use the shuttle service to go get something for dinner. They had sandwiches and lots of other options for us to choose from but we decided to go out because we wanted a hot meal. We picked a place that was open but was out of the area the shuttle went. But because the shuttle driver wasn't busy they took us to our dining place. The driver was excellent and we had a great conversation. As we were heading to our dining destination I could see so many other places open for business on Christmas Eve that were closer to our hotel. I felt bad that our driver had to go so far out of the way to accomidate our dining choice. Next time I will do a better search for restaurants closer to our hotel. The service was top
compliments: desk clerk was very nice and helpful with check-in
room was very clean and nice
shuttle driver accommodated dining request despite being out of the way

reviews.raw_text: This hotel is wonderful. We were in town for a wedding. It was only a 15 min. drive to the venue. They have on site parking for $20. Price of room was reasonable. The staff was very friendly. When we checked in, they gave us each a complimentary chocolate chip cookie, warm in the bag, it was delicious 😋.  Also bottles of water. The room was clean and the beds were very comfortable. I would definitely recommend this hotel. If I ever need to stay in Madison again, I will book here.

reviews.raw_text: Great stay overall. Parking was easy and felt very safe. Check-in was quick, and the front desk staff were friendly and efficient. reviews.raw_text: The only reason I didn’t give this place a 5 star is because of the tiny, dated pool and hot tub. Service was excellent. Rooms were clean and comfortable. The whole hotel smelled great and had a cozy atmosphere even in the lobby. The location had a great view and the neighborhood is beautiful, walkable & easy to navigate. My daughter and I were in town overnight for a concert at the Majestic Theater. The venue was a few minutes walk away, as well as restaurants, a few bars & the waterfront. We will definitely be  back next time we’re in town! 😊
complaints: tiny, dated pool and hot tub

reviews.raw_text: Overall it's not a bad hotel. I used it when traveling to Madison for work. The first time I stayed I had a nice little suite overlooking the lake. The second time I stayed I had a king bed with some questionable splatter marks on the headboard. Since my third stay was already pre-booked through work, out I went again, but got a different room with two queen beds overlooking the capitol building. The parking garage onsite has high ceilings, I believe 6'11", so larger vehicles can fit inside easily. The restaurant service is SLOW. I ended up ordering to-go or getting doordash/uber eats while I was there. Would I stay there again, probably, but I would not pick that king bed room ever again 😆

reviews.raw_text:
Came to the Hilton Monona Terrace in Madison for an overnight stay and was greeted to a lovely suite as well as a phenomenal executive lounge. The welcoming staff not only created an, inviting experience, but provided helping assistance along the way.

I would highly recommend staying here again. A couple of the great highlights are the great views of the lake as well as the state capital!

Super nice hotel, clean, room was comfortable. So nice having digital check in/out, didn't have to wait at the desk for anything. It was really a nice setup.

Word of caution, the parking structure onsite is not friendly to larger vehicles. So, love the hotel, parking sucks for big vehicles. Would rather you guys work out some deal with the public structure across the street or wherever the nearest is and for full size vehicles allow us to use that instead.

complaints: Heater fan is always on and noisy

reviews.raw_text:
Clean hotel. Friendly staff. Nice pool and hot tub. I enjoyed my stay like usual at the hilton hotels.
Good area for finding a place to eat. And breakfast was good. The usual hilton chain breakfast I've learned to look forward to. I highly recommend this hotel.
compliments: Good area for finding a place to eat

reviews.raw_text:
It was Great, everything i have come to expect from Hilton, Hilton has high standards and this hotel definitely hits the mark. You will notice a modern appearance as you enter the hotel with swing (pretty close to hanging egg chairs) chairs. There is a nice business area, small alcove, sundries shop, 24/7 fitness center, this is the first hotel I have seen with a few games in the lobby area, pool and what appeared to be a Super Chexx Pro bubble hockey table. They have a welcoming pet area and the parking lot was mostly clear of snow, (for reference it just snowed, and the hotel was prompt on clean up.

My room was great, nothing to complain about, clean bed, clean floors, AC/Heater works, amazing shower. Nice ironing board and iron on the room. .
compliments: Prompt snow cleanup in parking lot

reviews.raw_text:
I really loved this place. It’s especially perfect for people who enjoy a quiet environment, want 24-hour gym access, or need a calm space to use their phone, laptop, or get work/studying done.

The lobby is very well designed, with a pool table and game tables, and there are charging outlets everywhere, which is incredibly convenient. Everything is spotlessly clean, making the whole space feel comfortable and relaxing.

In addition to the lobby, I really appreciated the 24-hour fitness center. It has all the essential equipment and fully meets basic workout needs during travel.

Another highlight was the in-room amenities—the body wash, hand soap, shampoo, and body lotion are all in easy-to-use pump bottles. They’re clean, neat, and very convenient, and you can control the amount you use yourself.

Parking was also very easy, with plenty of spaces right at the entrance, which made everything even more convenient.

Overall, a very comfortable, well-thought-out, and user-friendly stay.
compliments: In-room amenities in convenient pump bottles
Easy parking with plenty of spaces

reviews.raw_text: Newer facility. Nice, clean rooms. No swimming pool. Excellent service. Breakfast was well done. The only negative is an hourly charge for late check-out.
complaints: Hourly charge for late check-out
No swimming pool

reviews.raw_text:
Close to the beltline (US 14) on the west side of Madison. A somewhat small guest room with two queen beds, in nice condition. The bathroom was a nice modern room in good condition as well. There is a mini refrigerator in the room, but not a coffee maker or microwave. Coffee and tea are available in the lobby area. The room TV was more than adequate for us.

Breakfast: Cold Cereals, hot oatmeal, pancakes (if the automatic machine is working), toast, juices, bread & bagels, hard-boiled eggs, bacon, and small premade omelets. There is also a not so obvious microwave provided in the breakfast area to heat items if needed.

Overall, I felt this hotel was an excellent value and wouldn't hesitate to stay here when in the Madison area. As it is approximately 30 min from the Dane County/Madison airport, I'd probably stay someplace closer to the airport if an early flight is planned.
complaints: Small guest room
No coffee maker or microwave in room
Not obvious microwave in breakfast area
30 minutes from airport

ai_summary: SpringHill Suites Madison consistently wows guests with its spotless rooms, thoughtful amenities, and genuinely warm staff — frequently described as delivering a five-star feel from a non-five-star brand. Minor drawbacks like paid parking and occasional bar closures are barely a footnote against an otherwise near-flawless guest experience.

reviews.raw_text: My husband and I stayed here on a weekend away. I travel quite a bit for work and stay in many hotels throughout the year. This hotel is so nice! It is clean, quiet, and feels brand new. They also thought of all the details - the things you miss when you’re on the road. Examples - an ice and distilled water machine on each floor for ice water, a big beautiful gym with a separate area for floor work, very nice lobby and breakfast area, giant windows in the rooms, chocolates upon checkin, optional climate controlled indoor parking, friendly staff, complimentary earbuds in the gym, guest laundry (how many times I’ve wanted this on the road!) This hotel is walking distance to a great mall and a short ride to downtown. As I compared prices to this hotel vs downtown hotels, I’d stay here every time! Loved it!
compliments: thoughtful details like ice and distilled water machine on each floor
big beautiful gym with separate floor work area

reviews.raw_text: I stayed here while my wife had a multi-day stay at UW Hospital. The hotel is new and very well maintained and clean. The breakfast options are nice and the restaurant and bar (closed while I was there in early Jan) look good too. The location is amazing! It's across the street from a Super Target, Metcalf's grocery store, and the Hilldale mall, so there is a lot to do during an extended stay and many good restaurants are close by. The heated parking ramp is a nice luxury during the winter as well. The staff was friendly and very helpful with a few odd requests that I had. I highly recommend this hotel, especially if you are staying more than a couple nights.
compliments: excellent location across from Target and mall
heated parking ramp a nice luxury

reviews.raw_text: I must say, this is the best hotel I’ve ever stayed in the Madison area. Despite having tried over a dozen hotels in Madison, this one stands out as the absolute best. The staff was incredibly friendly and attentive, and the rooms were spotless and well-maintained. The complimentary breakfast was fine, and the parking was a bonus at $25, with the added convenience of in-and-out privileges. The location was also perfect, making it easy to explore the city and create a memorable weekend.
compliments: best hotel in the Madison area

reviews.raw_text: I can't say enough great things about Isabel! Our family of 4 arrived on a Sunday evening, after a long, lovely day in Madison, cheering on the Women's Volleyball team and walking around campus. Upon arrival at the hotel, she promptly greeted us in a most friendly manner and made us feel quite welcome. Better yet, she took care of us and upgraded us to a suite. It was the most lovely, simple gesture that made a world of difference. We greatly appreciated the extra space and her thoughtfulness! She's a fantastic member of your staff and deserves accolades and a raise!
compliments: staff member Isabel was friendly and welcoming

reviews.raw_text:
How am I supposed to stay at a "normal" hotel after staying here? Honestly gives that 5 star vibe from a non-5 star hotel chain. Only problem was the freaking packers game was on peacock which, through contractual issues I'm sure, the hotel couldn't display on their TVs at the bar. Had to jury rig an agreement with another guest to watch it with him on his iPad. But thats more a commentary on the issues with NFL broadcasting rights than with the hotel.
Great time though.
complaints: Packers game unavailable on bar TV due to Peacock streaming rights
compliments: gives 5 star vibe from non-5 star hotel chain

reviews.raw_text:
We had to travel to Madison to take our dog to the UW school of veterinary medicine. We chose the Graduate hotel because they are pet friendly. This was by far the best pet friendly hotel we have stayed at. The decor is so thoughtful and the furniture is very comfortable and functional. It’s easy to feel at home in this kind of place. The room we stayed in was a corner room with a king bed, a desk, and a side chair. There was a floor to ceiling window that our dog enjoyed looking out of when she was recovering from dental work. The location is by Greek row so although there are a lot of grass options to walk your dog to, be cautious if you have a dog that likes to eat trash because there was kind of a lot of party trash along the sidewalks and yards.

We would stay here again and would recommend the hotel to anyone traveling to Madison.
compliments: best pet friendly hotel we have stayed at
furniture is very comfortable and functional

reviews.raw_text: If you’re doing business on campus, this is the spot. The Graduate Madison is charming and full of local character. Though the footprint is smaller than some Graduates, the service is massive. Amelia handled our check-in and was incredibly helpful, specifically regarding our Hilton Gold status benefits. It’s rare to find someone so knowledgeable and welcoming. We’re Hilton-only travelers, and this stay solidified that choice. Highly recommend!
compliments: charming and full of local character

reviews.raw_text: The room is tiny, there is no closet or other place to put clothing. It’s clearly a not very well renovated closet. $400/night, plus $15 “resort fee” but nothing to purchase except crappy cocktails. This is awful experience. Website misleading as to room amenities.  And the HVaC in the room sounds like a train. Don’t stay here. Bathroom has no counter. Hotel rooms in NYC are larger.  Walls are paper thin. Can hear very word of the conversation the room next door is having. When I complained, the front desk offered earplugs. How about- sound proofing? Almost forgot - the sheets are torn. Awful. A total rip off.

The room I got had poor ventilation and upon requesting to change to a better room, I was moved to a similar room just few rooms down, again with same alley facing view without good ventilation and on top of it, it had UNACCEPTABLE hygiene concerns (see below).

After walking up and down a few different streets to confirm that our car was parked where we thought it was, we went inside the hotel to ask if they had seen any tow trucks recently. After confirming that there had been a tow truck, we spoke to (who I’m assuming) was the manager working at the time. He explained to us that we were parked, along with one other car, in a spot that had been reserved for a hotel bus. I would say that we were very polite and partially confused in the beginning of the interaction. We were just happy to know the car hadn’t been stolen, but we were still curious to know why it had been towed. I brought up our concern that the sign didn’t say that the spot was no parking at the time of this incident. ai_summary: The Governors Mansion Inn is a beautifully maintained historic B&B in Madison's Mansion Hill neighborhood, praised almost universally for its spotless cleanliness, walkable location, and warm ambiance. reviews.raw_text: My friend and I stayed here on a Sunday night. We had tickets to a concert at the Orpheum Theatre. We arrived with clear instructions on where to park and how to enter the front/side door and our room. Although our room was small (we knew that when booking), it was very clean. Our room was a former servants quarters and had two twin beds that were very comfortable. The bathroom was clean, spacious and even had a little heater in it. We walked the half mile to the Orpheum and felt very safe. In the morning, there was a self serve breakfast of coffee/tea, scones, bagels and cream cheese, fruit, cheese and oatmeal. We were pleased with the variety and care in which it was presented. After breakfast we walked to the dock, thanks to the suggestions in the room. We would definitely stay here again and would recommend to others.
compliments: good variety of self-serve breakfast items

reviews.raw_text: Love it in January, when things are quiet and cold. The Mansion feels warm, inviting, like you're settling into a good book and each page you turn to has something interesting to keep your attention. Perfect location - walkable to everything you might want, but complete with view and access to the lake. The service staff is awesome.
compliments: view and access to the lake

reviews.raw_text:
We were in Madison for a concert and bad weather moved in. Our daughter got us a room at the Mansion. We weren't sure of what to expect, having only been to one b&b before and didn't enjoy it.

Our room was so clean and welcoming. We couldn't get the fireplace to cooperate but that was ok as the room was a bit warm for us. Glad I packed a little fan!

reviews.raw_text: Absolutely perfect. Great location, so so clean. David was extremely accommodating, as we had to make multiple modifications to the reservation due to winter weather and travel delays.

Pros: Great location, in the beautiful Mansion Hill area, close to Capitol Hill, lakes, and UW campus. It's a beautiful old home with lots of history and is well-appointed with antiques in the rooms. Bathrooms have been renovated recently, and it's quiet.

Cons: This place looks like a traditional B&B, and yet, we are instructed to let ourselves in the house and in our room because there is no host. There was someone there serving breakfast, but he really didn't play a host role. Despite it being this very historic home, the way it's appointed gives it kind of a sterile feel, which seemed a bit off. Lastly, the mattress needs replacing in the room where I slept - very saggy.

Would I stay there again?: Probably not. I think there are better choices in the area. I stayed at the Mansion Hill Inn last summer, just a few doors down the street, and it was truly exceptional.

reviews.raw_text:
I’m local to downtown Madison and regularly have friends and family stay at nearby hotels when they visit, so I’m pretty familiar with most of the downtown options. This Embassy Suites is genuinely one of the best hotels in town.

Location-wise, it’s hard to beat. You’re less than a block from King Street, with great bars and restaurants that skew more “non-university” (a bit more upscale). If you want more of the college bar/restaurant vibe, State Street is about a five-minute walk through Capitol Square. Despite being so central, it’s also surprisingly quiet. Most of the surrounding buildings are residential, so you’re not dealing with a loud bar scene outside your window until 2 a.m.

As of the time of writing, it’s one of the newer downtown properties, and the team has done a fantastic job maintaining it.

reviews.raw_text:
I recently stayed at the DoubleTree in Madison. Overall, it was a decent stay, but it did not feel like a four star hotel.

The room itself was clean and functional, but very standard. It felt comparable to a typical Holiday Inn or Hilton rather than a luxury property. There were no standout features in the room, and there was no complimentary breakfast, which I would expect at this level.

The room was also fairly loud at times. I am not sure if it was a vent system or the refrigerator, but there was noticeable background noise that made it less comfortable.

That said, the hotel does have a nice pool and a solid workout area, which were definite positives. The staff was also excellent, very polite, professional, and helpful throughout the stay. Plenty of food options in the area as well.

Overall, it was a good, standard hotel experience, but it did not meet my expectations for what I consider a true four star property.

reviews.raw_text:
Our evening flight out of Madison was canceled after 6+ hours of delays due to a snow storm, leaving us with no other option than to find accommodations for the night nearby. We chose this on a whim and arrived in an understandably sour mood.

This was the oasis we needed. The premises were clean and fairly impressive for the booking price. The attendant manning the front desk at ~1AM when we arrived was cheerful, courteous, and helpful. Our room was clean and well appointed. Breakfast in the morning was good and had shockingly attentive servers.

While we only stayed for a brief ~7 hours, my spouse and I were extremely pleased with our stay. If you find yourself in the area for leisure or similarly find yourself stuck in the middle of the night due to a canceled flight like we were, this is the place.

Discriminatory Treatment, Emotional Harm, and Policy Concerns

housekeeping requests ignored for days during extended stays
ai_summary: Home2 Suites Madison East shows promise as a newer, visually appealing property but is let down by persistent operational failures in staffing, housekeeping, and breakfast service. Guests on extended stays and those with reservations face consistent issues with responsiveness and follow-through, making it difficult to recommend despite its physical appeal.

reviews.raw_text:
As a loyal Hilton Honors member who frequently stays at Home2 Suites properties nationwide, I was extremely disappointed with my recent stay at Home2 Suites Madison East.

Check in was disorganized and the associate initially could not locate my reservation, even asking if it was under a completely different name after I had already provided my full name. I was also not offered the complimentary water typically extended to Honors members and had to return to request it.

The most concerning issue was breakfast service. The breakfast area was severely unorganized and poorly maintained. Multiple food items were empty and never replenished. There were no forks available and no standard cups stocked. Guests were using the small waffle batter cups to drink juice. Dirty dishes were piled up and the area appeared unmanaged.

Several guests, including myself, waited 15 to 20 minutes to see if items would be restocked. Nothing changed. Many guests left the property to purchase breakfast elsewhe
complaints: Check-in disorganized, reservation not located
Complimentary water for Honors members not offered
Breakfast area severely unorganized with empty food items never replenished
No forks or standard cups available, dirty dishes piled up

reviews.raw_text: Don't plan on a quick check in experience and DEFINITELY skip the free breakfast. Long line to check in, only 1 person working, room wasn't ready for us, then gave us a room we didn't book. Everything in the breakfast area was chaos. No juice (the staff said its broken), they ran out of eggs and sausage (Staff person said she didn't even know what to say). Ran out of coffee creamer. The dirty dish pile was overflowing from a cart parked by the coffee station. Disgusting. Oh, and we requested dishwasher detergent for the room last night and still have not received any.
complaints: Long check-in line with only 1 person working
Room not ready, given wrong room type
Breakfast area chaos with no juice, ran out of eggs and sausage
Ran out of coffee creamer, overflowing dirty dish pile, dishwasher detergent request unfulfilled

reviews.raw_text: The place was beautiful! Immaculate. The bed was soooo comfortable. Plus the lounge and pool areas were lively. Very nice stay. The staff was super great.
compliments: Lounge and pool areas were lively

reviews.raw_text: Nice new hotel for extended stays, decent breakfast, good location with plenty of food and shopping options
compliments: Nice new hotel for extended stays
Good location with plenty of food and shopping options

reviews.raw_text: I had a stay of 17 nights for work. Requested a room straighten and towels on day 3, that did not happen until day 5. After that there was 8 days between my next cleaning even though was promised every day it would be handled. I was using pool towels to shower. No covers to take a cup of coffee on the road for the first 10 days as well. Did reply to the text message asking how my stay was with a return message asking best time to call after my reply no futher contact was made. The hotel was obviously new and very up to date. Not a positive experience here.
complaints: Requested room straighten and towels on day 3, not completed until day 5
8 days between cleanings despite promise of daily service
Used pool towels to shower due to lack of clean towels
No coffee cup covers for first 10 days, follow-up contact from hotel abandoned
compliments: Hotel was new and very up to date
